key_file_excerpts..github/workflows/gh-pages.yml:
name: gh-pages

on:
  push:
    branches: ["master"]
  workflow_dispatch: {}

permissions:
  contents: write

concurrency:
  group: "docs-gh-pages"
  cancel-in-progress: false

jobs:
  deploy:
    if: ${{ github.event_name != 'push' || !contains(github.event.head_commit.message, '[skip docs]') }}
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout master
        uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: master
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v5
        with:
          node-version: "24"

      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v6
        with:
          python-version: "3.12"

      - name: Install build dependencies
        run: |
          sudo apt-get update
          sudo apt-get install -y pandoc
          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
          python -m pip install pyyaml
          npm install

      - name: Build canonical Antora source tree
        run: |
          python3 tools/build_antora_site.py

      - name: Build site with Antora
        run: |
          npx antora antora-playbook.yml --fetch

      - name: Copy public site extras
        run: |
          if [ -f docs/CNAME ]; then
            cp docs/CNAME .site_build/CNAME
          fi
          if [ -d assets ]; then
            mkdir -p .site_build/assets
            cp -R assets/. .site_build/assets/
          fi
          touch .site_build/.nojekyll

      - name: Publish owner-facing site to gh-pages
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git remote set-url origin "https://x-access-token:${GITHUB_TOKEN}@github.com/${GITHUB_REPOSITORY}.git"

          workdir="$(mktemp -d)"

          if git ls-remote --exit-code origin gh-pages >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            git fetch origin gh-pages:gh-pages
            git worktree add --force "$workdir" gh-pages
          else
            git worktree add --detach "$workdir"
            (
              cd "$workdir"
              git checkout --orphan gh-pages
            )
          fi

          find "$workdir" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 ! -name '.git' -exec rm -rf {} +
          cp -R .site_build/. "$workdir"/

          (
            cd "$workdir"
            git add -A
            if [ -n "$(git status --porcelain)" ]; then
              git commit -m "docs: publish canonical Antora site"
              git push origin HEAD:gh-pages --force
            else
              echo "No site changes to publish."
            fi
          )

          git worktree remove "$workdir" --force || true
key_file_excerpts..github/workflows/quality-atlas-assessme:
name: quality-atlas-assessment

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      component:
        description: 'Optional component_id to assess (for example: documentating)'
        required: false
        type: string
      mode:
        description: 'Optional run mode override'
        required: false
        type: choice
        options:
          - auto
          - dry-run
          - responses
        default: auto
  schedule:
    - cron: '27 9 */2 * *'

permissions:
  contents: write

concurrency:
  group: quality-atlas-assessment
  cancel-in-progress: false

jobs:
  assess:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout master
        uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: master
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v6
        with:
          python-version: '3.12'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
          python -m pip install pyyaml

      - name: Seed current component snapshots
        run: |
          python3 tools/seed_quality_atlas_snapshots.py

      - name: Run scheduled assessment dry-run or AI-backed pass
        id: assessment
        env:
          OPENAI_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.OPENAI_API_KEY }}
          QUALITY_ATLAS_OPENAI_MODEL: ${{ vars.QUALITY_ATLAS_OPENAI_MODEL || 'gpt-5' }}
          INPUT_COMPONENT: ${{ github.event.inputs.component || '' }}
          INPUT_MODE: ${{ github.event.inputs.mode || 'auto' }}
        run: |
          mode="dry-run"
          if [ "${INPUT_MODE}" = "responses" ]; then
            mode="responses"
          elif [ "${INPUT_MODE}" = "dry-run" ]; then
            mode="dry-run"
          elif [ -n "${OPENAI_API_KEY}" ]; then
            mode="responses"
          fi

          component_args=""
          selected_component="ALL_ENABLED"
          if [ -n "${INPUT_COMPONENT}" ]; then
            component_args="--component ${INPUT_COMPONENT}"
            selected_component="${INPUT_COMPONENT}"
          fi

          echo "mode=${mode}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "component=${selected_component}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

          echo "Assessment mode=${mode} component=${selected_component}"
          python3 tools/run_quality_atlas_assessment.py --mode "$mode" ${component_args}
      - name: Print assessment errors
        if: always()
        run: |
          python3 - <<'PY'
          from pathlib import Path
          import json
          root = Path('.sync/quality-atlas/generated/assessment-runs')
          if not root.exists():
              print('No assessment run directory found.')
              raise SystemExit(0)
          runs = sorted([p for p in root.iterdir() if p.is_dir()])
          if not runs:
              print('No assessment runs found.')
              raise SystemExit(0)
          run_dir = runs[-1]
          print(f'Latest assessment run: {run_dir}')
          errors = sorted(run_dir.glob('*.error.json'))
          if not errors:
              print('No component error files found.')
              raise SystemExit(0)
          for path in errors:
              print(f'\n===== {path.name} =====')
              try:
                  payload = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding='utf-8'))
              except Exception:
                  print(path.read_text(encoding='utf-8', errors='ignore'))
                  continue
              compact = {
                  'component': payload.get('component'),
                  'title': payload.get('title'),
                  'mode': payload.get('mode'),
                  'error_type': payload.get('error_type'),
                  'error': payload.get('error'),
              }
              print(json.dumps(compact, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False))
          PY

      - name: Write assessment step summary
        if: always()
        run: |
          python3 - <<'PY'
          from pathlib import Path
          import json
          summary_path = Path('.sync/quality-atla

verdict.summary: Antora-driven documentation site with automated GitHub Pages publishing and scheduled Quality Atlas assessment; strong delivery scaffolding but limited automated content QA and minimal runtime/observability typical of static sites.
strengths: Automated site build and publish via GitHub Actions (Antora + gh-pages).
Clear content pipeline using Antora with search (lunr extension).
Scheduled Quality Atlas assessment workflow seeded and runnable.
Custom domain support present (CNAME) and nojekyll handling.
risks: Minimal automated content QA (no link/check steps observed in CI).
Single-environment deploy on push to gh-pages without previews/canary.
Operational assets (e.g., Cloudflare, Helm) exist but are not wired into CI.
Runtime monitoring/observability not defined for the published site.
Ownership/architecture boundaries for repo tooling are not documented.
next_actions: Add basic content quality gates to CI (broken-link validation and site checks) before publishing.
Introduce PR preview builds or ephemeral artifacts to review changes pre-production.
Wire existing .smoke probes into CI for post-build validations.
If Cloudflare/other edge is intended, add a CI step to publish/configure it and document the flow.
Document directory responsibilities and ownership in README/BUILDING to clarify boundaries.
Add a lightweight rollback strategy for gh-pages (retain and reference last-good commit).
notes: Scope is a documentation component; many runtime metrics are naturally less relevant.
Assessment is based on repository structure and CI workflows; no separate quality tool outputs were provided.
This component is bootstrap-enabled inside the atlas repository to provide a live local target.

product.summary: Antora-based static documentation site with a clear build/publish pipeline and custom domain, providing a solid identity for owner-facing docs.
risks: Content quality gates are not enforced automatically in CI.
Audience/scope for the docs is implicit rather than explicitly stated.
improvements: State the target audience and scope on the site landing/README.
Add pre-publish validation for content (e.g., link and structural checks).

architecture.summary: Repository shows organized tooling and content boundaries (.gate, .deploy, .smoke), but there is little application architecture to assess and responsibilities are not documented.
risks: Domain/bounded-context boundaries for repo tooling are not described.
Coupling between scripts/workflows is not documented, which may hinder maintenance.
improvements: Add a brief architecture/ownership section mapping directories to responsibilities in README or BUILDING.
Document how the Antora source, CI workflows, and publication artifacts relate.

runtime.summary: Static site runtime via GitHub Pages; scalability is largely handled by the platform, with no explicit runtime tuning or monitoring in the repo.
risks: No runtime health checks or synthetic probes are wired into the publishing workflow.
Edge/CDN configuration (e.g., Cloudflare) exists in repo but is not used in CI.
improvements: Document the hosting/edge strategy and expected caching behavior.
Integrate a simple post-publish probe step to verify site availability.
If Cloudflare is intended, add an automated deployment/configuration step.

qa.summary: Quality scaffolding exists (.gate, .smoke) but CI does not run automated tests or link/structure validation before publishing.
risks: Broken links or content regressions could ship without detection.
No contract/e2e checks exist for the published site artifacts.
improvements: Add link and content validation steps to the gh-pages workflow prior to publish.
Wire .smoke probes or minimal checks to run on each build.
Introduce a basic PR check that builds the site and surfaces issues early.

ops.summary: CI automates site build and gh-pages publish with concurrency control, and a scheduled assessment job maintains Quality Atlas snapshots.
risks: Single production deploy on push without preview or staged environments.
No explicit rollback mechanism is defined for gh-pages publishes.
improvements: Add PR previews or ephemeral deployments for review.
Keep a last-known-good gh-pages reference and document rollback steps.
Cache dependencies in CI to stabilize and speed up builds.

market.summary: As a documentation component, market alignment is secondary; the site emphasizes documentation quality and search via the Antora lunr extension.
risks: Audience and value proposition are not clearly stated for external readers.
No visible changelog/release notes to communicate documentation updates.
improvements: Add a short 'Who this is for' section on the landing page/README.
Publish a simple docs changelog or release notes section in the site.

overall.summary: Solid automated documentation delivery with scheduled assessment support; primary gaps are automated content QA and staged delivery practices.
risks: Potential to ship regressions due to lack of pre-publish checks.
Operational assets remain unintegrated, creating drift between intent and execution.
improvements: Introduce CI quality gates and previews before publishing.
Connect smoke/probes post-build and document ownership/rollback paths.
